methods.name: Peer-Median

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Bone AI is a seed-stage defense and security company that has raised $12M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comparable universe of 10 companies (2 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector and stage, including peers such as Tenna Systems ($60M), Ares Industries ($150M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Stark Defence ($500M), and Scale AI ($29.0B).

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $12M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $30M. The peer-median method examines five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $300M, though the peer set exhibits substantial dispersion (range: $60M–$29.0B), reflecting heterogeneity in defense-tech market positioning and maturity.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $26M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $165M. The wide dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty regarding Bone AI's market position relative to a peer set that spans from early-stage defense platforms to later-stage, well-capitalized competitors. Key unknowns include the company's proprietary technology differentiation, existing customer traction, and path to meaningful revenue—factors that would typically justify positioning toward either end of the derived range.
